name: Ballew, William
motto: "Thoughts even more than overt acts, reveal character."
description: A very dignified young man, whose exceptionally good conduct in school might serve as a model for all who in their turn shall follow him. He will make a good farmer. A good thinker.

name: Roberts, Luverta
high school: C. S. H. S., Frankfort, Ky
motto: "Industry keeps the body healthy, the mind clear, the heart whole, and the purse full."
description: She has a sweet disposition and many, many friends. An excellent student, very fond of Domestic Art.

name: Hogan, Elizabeth
motto: "Except a living man, there is nothing more wonderful than books! A message to us from the dead—from human souls we never saw, who lived, perhaps, thousands of miles away. And yet these, in those little sheets of paper, speak to us, arouse us, terrify us, teach us, comfort us, open our hearts to us as brothers."
description: One of the leaders of her class.

name: Blandford, Ollie
motto: "Slow but Sure."
description: Evidently she thinks, "It is better either to be silent, or say things of more value than silence. Sooner throw a pearl at hazard than an idle or useless word; and do not say a little in many words, but a great deal in a few."

name: Summers, Robert W.
motto: "True dignity is never gained by place and never lost when honors are withdrawn."
description: A very accomplished, good natured, and obliging young man. An all round student, especially in Latin.

name: Jones, Emma L.
motto: "Contentment is a pearl of great price, and whoever procures it at the expense of ten thousand desires makes a wise and happy purchase."
description: Very studious indeed. A great conversationalist. Another "Belle" of the class. Emma is an earnest working girl.

name: Turner, Lillard
motto: "There is no genius in life like the genius of energy and activity."
description: Turner is an excellent student. A great historian. He is energetic and earnest. Talks to the girls occasionally. He is a business young man and will make good in future life—judging from what he has done during his stay at Normal.

name: Turner, Hattie
motto: "The grandest operations, both in nature and grace, are the most silent and imperceptible. The shallow brook babbles in its passage and is heard by everyone; but the coming of the season is silent and unseen."
description: Very dignified and modest. An excellent student.

name: Brooks, William
motto: "Heaven's eternal wisdom has decreed, that man should ever stand in need of man."
description: Brooks has proven himself to be one of the dependable men of the school. If you want anything done and done right, call on Brooks. He is a good student and liked by all.

name: Waters, Lenora V.
motto: "Great souls, by nature half divine, soar to the stars, and hold a near acquaintance with the gods."
description: Former student of State University. A very competent young lady. She has a lovely disposition and is loved by all. She is glad to be relieved from the joys and sorrows of a student's life. She is very ambitious.

name: Roberts, Charles
motto: "Ambition is a germ from which all growth of nobleness proceeds."
description: Very popular young man. An excellent extemporaneous speaker. A lover of truth. (Full of fun.) Valedictorian of his class. Ambitious.

name: Stoner, Mary E.
motto: "Genuine and innocent wit is surely the flavor of the mind. Man could not direct his way by plain reason, and support his life by tasteless food; but God has given him wit, and brightness, and laughter, and perfumes to enliven the days of man's pilgrimage, and to charm his pained steps over the burning marl."
description: Has much literary ability and a gifted wit. Very sweet tempered. Good in all her studies.

name: Adams, Mary C.
motto: "Well arranged time is the surest mark of a well arranged mind."
description: Graduate of Russell High School, Lexington, Kentucky. Finishes Special Course in Domestic Science. Has much talent.

name: Reeves, Jeanette
motto: "Never argue while eating, the person who is not hungry will get the best of the argument."
description: Graduate of Winchester High School. One of the jolly girls of the class. We have often wondered if "Ruby" is her birth stone. She has a very serious look, but looks are sometimes deceiving. She likes to argue, especially while eating; she should remember the saying. Will make an excellent teacher.

name: Braxton, Alberta
description: Treasurer of the Sunday School, and very trusty, of course. She has been with us so long, we will miss her very much. She has an excellent alto voice.

name: Hughes, Margaret
motto: "Nothing but the right shall ever be expedient, since that can never be true expediency which would sacrifice a greater good to a less."
description: A very modest young lady with many friends. Wonder what she will do when Agnes leaves her? Has an excellent alto voice.

name: Bird, Grover
motto: "The chiefest action for a man of spirit is never to be out of action; the soul was never put into the body to stand still."
description: Bird is a real scholar, a good man, and a hard worker. He pursued three courses during his stay here—receives diplomas in two of them.

name: Fleming, Agnes
motto: "The art of pleasing consists in being pleased. To be amiable is to be satisfied with one's self and others."
description: "Blessed is the person who can do something to please her," nevertheless, we love her.

name: Johnson, Clarence
high school: C. S. H. S., Frankfort, Ky.
motto: "A life that will bear the inspection of men and God is the only certificate of true religion."
description: An all-round student. Very much liked by the girls of his class. He has a pleasant word and smile for everyone. Will make a good preacher.

name: Gratts, Georgia E.
motto: "True popularity is not the popularity which is followed after, but the popularity which follows after."
description: A lover of instrumental music. Gets serious now and then. Dear Little Girl. Has an excellent alto voice. An all-round good student.

name: Nichols, Minnie
motto: "Refinement creates beauty everywhere. It is the grossness of the spectator that discovers anything like grossness in the object."
description: A very quiet young lady who knows how to make friends and keep them.

name: Glass, Rosa
motto: "Bashfulness may sometimes exclude pleasure, but seldom opens any avenue to sorrow or remorse."
description: Poor Rosa! She does not believe there is any such thing as a monosyllable. Has a sweet voice.

name: Banks, Etta Ray
high school: C. S. H. S., Frankfort, Ky.
motto: "Is one of the fairest and most glorious gifts of God to which Satan is a bitter enemy, for it moves from the heart the weight of sorrow and the fascination of evil thoughts,"
description: A very pleasant young lady who has many friends. She has the accomplishment, which as Luther says,—music.

name: Butler, Malinda
motto: "An hour's industry will do more to produce cheerfulness, suppress evil humours, and retrieve one's affairs, than a month's moaning. It sweetens enjoyments and seasons our attainments with a delightful relish."
clubs: Teacher of Sunday School Class No. 1
description: Very quiet and pleasant. A general favorite. With her.

name: Bate, Langston
motto: "He who labors diligently need never despair; for all things are accomplished by diligence and labor."
description: The girls like to pet him. He might be a baby in age and size, but he certainly is not in brains. Diligence is his watchword.

name: Haskins, Elizabeth
motto: "A good disposition is more valuable than gold; for the latter is a gift of fortune, but the former the dower of nature."
description: An exceptionally good essayist and speaker in general. Another leader of the class. Class essayist. Salutatorian.

name: Bacon, McKinley
motto: "A proud man never shows his pride so much as when he is civil."
description: Fine fellow—liked by all. Very courteous.

name: Hambleton, Alice
motto: "Peace does not dwell in outward things, but within our soul we may deserve it in the midst of the bitterest pain, if our will remains firm and submissive. Peace in this life springs from acquiescence, not in an exemption from suffering."
description: A former student of State University, Louisville, Ky. An excellent student. One of the bells of her class.

name: Wright, Eva
motto: "The avarice of power; and happiness herself is soon sacrificed to that very lust of dominion which was first encouraged only as the best means of obtaining it."
description: An excellent student. Very witty. Likes to shop. Very ambitious.

name: Wilson, Charlotte
motto: "Providence has nothing good or high in store for one who does not resolutely aim at something high or good. A purpose is the eternal condition of success."
description: Very sweet tempered. An excellent student with high ideals.

name: Booker, Alberta
motto: "Be rather wise than witty, for much wit has commonly much froth, and it is hard to jest and not sometimes jeer too, which many times sinks deeper than was intended or expected, and what was designed for mirth ends in sadness."
description: Fine in music. Ardent worker. Alberta is one of our serious minded girls.

name: Rhodes, Meacie
motto: "I will govern my life and thoughts as if the world were to see the one and hate the other; for what does it signify to make anything a secret to my neighbor, when to God all our privates are open?"
description: A very modest and industrious young lady with high ideals. Stands well in her class. Always has a kind word for everyone.

name: Boyer, Benjamin
motto: "A grateful thought toward heaven is of itself a prayer."
description: A dear good boy. He is a serious and quiet fellow. Good in Methods. With his manly disposition, he is sure to make good in life.

name: Fields, Mary B.
motto: "Excitement is so engraven on our nature that it may be regarded an appetite; and like all other appetites, it is not sinful unless indulged to excess."
description: Happy disposition—sings night and morning. Masters Latin with ease.

name: Campbell, Preston
motto: "The greatest events of an age are its best thoughts. Thought finds its way into action."
description: A Farmer, par excellence. Campbell is a young man who will make his mark in life. Industrious, persevering, and thoughtful.

name: Taylor, Allene
motto: "A beautiful smile is to the female countenance what the sunbeam is to the landscape; it embellishes an inferior face, and redeems an ugly one."
description: Very studious and jolly. Talented Soloist. Takes great interest in Agriculture and Domestic Art. We hope that when she shall have finished here she will be so fortunate as to get a position in the kindergarten, for her height forbids her attempting to teach elsewhere.

name: Williams, Emma B.
motto: "If you have talents, industry will improve them; if moderate abilities, industry will supply their deficiencies. Nothing is denied to well-directed labor; nothing is ever to be attained without it."
description: Very quiet and modest. All things in her possession are "Touch me nots". As practice makes perfect, she will make an excellent collector. That which we mostly admire in her, is she believes that.

name: Turner, Anita
motto: "Know the true value of time; snatch, seize, and enjoy every moment of it. No idleness, no delay, no procrastination; never put off till to-morrow what you can do to-day."
description: Has changed considerably since her first year in school. Fine speaker. Very fond of reading and singing.

name: Greavious, Rosa
motto: "Good nature is the beauty of the mind, and like personal beauty, wins almost without anything else—sometimes indeed, in spite of positive deficiencies."
description: Graduate of Russell High School which makes her an ideal young lady. Stands well in her class. The Mozart Society would be a complete failure without her excellent alto voice.

name: Berry, Martha
motto: "Let your religion be seen. Lamps do not talk, but they do shine. A lighthouse sounds no drums, it beats no gong; yet, far over the waters, its friendly light is seen by the mariners."
description: A great church worker. Her advice to all is.

name: Hayes, John
high school: C. S. H. S., Frankfort, Ky
motto: "As charity covers a multitude of sins before God; so does politeness before men."
description: Graduate of the C. S. H. S., Frankfort, Ky. He has made good with teachers and students. He is one of the students of whom the Institution is proud. Never offends.

name: Garrett, Lucile
motto: "Blessed is the man who first invented sleep. It covers him all over, thoughts and all, like a cloak. It is meat for the hungry, drink for the thirsty, heat for the cold, and cold for the hot. It makes the shepherd equal to the monarch, and the fool equal to the wise. There is but one evil in it, and that is, it resembles death, since between a dead and sleeping man there is but little difference."
description: Lucile has an excellent intellect, and is one of our sweetest singers.

name: King, Rufus
motto: "How easy to be amiable in the midst of happiness and success."
description: King is the "Chesterfield" of the Hill. Never off his "base," but ever cool and calm. Very popular.

name: Story, Alma
motto: "As to matters of dress, she would recommend that one never be first in the fashion nor the last out of it."
description: Was once quiet, but it did not afford her the pleasure she was looking for, so she pursued the opposite course, not to an extreme, however. Very fond of reading. Very good in her class work.

name: Shoffner, Chloe
high school: Wendell Phillips High School, Chicago, Ill
motto: "Let all your veins in life be directed to a solid, however moderate, independence; for without it no man can be happy, not even honest."
description: Graduate of Wendell Phillips High School, Chicago, Ill. She is very studious, and has exceptional ability for giving absurd answers. We all love Chloe.

name: Taylor, Laura
motto: "A cheerful temper; joined with innocence, will make beauty attractive, knowledge delightful, and wit good-natured. It will lighten sickness, poverty, and affection; convert ignorance into an amiable simplicity, and render deformity itself agreeable."
description: Class Prophet. Inclined to be witty like her roommate—Mary. An excellent student.

name: Berry, Mary
high school: C. S. H. S., Frankfort, Ky
motto: "Modesty is a shining light; it prepares the mind to receive knowledge and the heart for truth."
description: Graduate of C. S. H. S., Frankfort, Ky. Mary is one of the girls who by her kindness and modesty has won a warm spot in the hearts of her teachers and schoolmates.

name: Simmons, Hattie
motto: "A just and reasonable modesty does not only recommend eloquence, but sets off every great talent of which a man can be possessed. It heightens all the virtues which it accompanies; like the shades in paintings, it raises and rounds every figure and makes the colors more beautiful though not so glaring as they would be without it."
description: Graduate of Lincoln Institute, Simpsonville, Ky. Fine musician. Very modest.
